final_prompt.prompt:
Create an original, non-infringing gongbi heavy-color handscroll painting depicting the Lantern Festival panorama in Tang Dynasty Chang'an city.

Style/medium: gongbi heavy-color (工笔重彩) handscroll (长卷) painting, Tang Dynasty court painting aesthetic
Composition: wide horizontal panoramic scroll layout, sweeping cityscape from left to right, layered depth with foreground crowds and background city gates and towers
Subject: Tang Dynasty Chang'an city during the Lantern Festival (元宵灯会), tens of thousands of flower lanterns (万盏花灯) filling the scene — palace lanterns, lotus lanterns, dragon lanterns, silk lanterns hung on trees and archways, crowds of Tang-era citizens in period costume celebrating
Scene/backdrop: Chang'an night cityscape — Zhuque Avenue, city gates, timber-frame pavilions and towers, willow trees strung with lanterns, night sky illuminated by lantern glow
Lighting/mood: warm golden luminescence radiating from ten-thousand lanterns, night sky transitioning from deep indigo to amber near the light sources, festive and magnificent
Color palette: rich mineral pigments — vermilion, azurite, malachite green, gold, deep indigo night sky, warm amber lantern glow
Materials/textures: silk scroll texture, fine-line gongbi brushwork with meticulous detail on lantern patterns and garment embroidery, mineral pigment layering
Text (verbatim): 
Constraints: horizontal handscroll (长卷) aspect ratio, Tang Dynasty architectural accuracy, gongbi line quality throughout, no anachronistic elements
Avoid: ink-wash (水墨) loose brushwork, modern lighting effects, Western perspective projection, Song or Ming Dynasty architectural styles
